title: Local Google Ads MCP package with mixed credential / non-credential env keys (REVIEW)

origin.description: Single-server config from the public MCP catalog repo khopilot/amazing-mcp-for-productivity (productivity/google_ads_mcp.json). A local 'npx -y google-ads-mcp-server' launcher with five GOOGLE_ADS_* env keys. The repo carries no detectable license, so only the non-copyrightable config shape is retained. All committed env values are already upstream's own placeholders ('your_google_ads_...'); no real secret was present.
redactions: license omitted: source repo has no detectable license; stored as a shape-preserving redacted snapshot, not redistributed verbatim
no secret redaction needed: all five GOOGLE_ADS_* env values were already upstream placeholders ('your_google_ads_*')

humanExpectation.rationale: A local launcher with credential-shaped env keys (GOOGLE_ADS_CLIENT_SECRET, GOOGLE_ADS_DEVELOPER_TOKEN, GOOGLE_ADS_REFRESH_TOKEN -> 'secrets.env-key') plus an unpinned package ('npx -y google-ads-mcp-server' -> 'supply.unpinned-package'). The honest verdict is REVIEW: the credential surface must be confirmed sourced safely, and the package pinned. The value of this case is the MIXED env shape — it pins that the secrets detector flags only the credential-shaped names and leaves the two non-credential ones (GOOGLE_ADS_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_ADS_CUSTOMER_ID) unflagged.
knownLimitations: secrets.env-key keys on credential-shaped substrings (SECRET / TOKEN); it flags GOOGLE_ADS_CLIENT_SECRET, GOOGLE_ADS_DEVELOPER_TOKEN, GOOGLE_ADS_REFRESH_TOKEN but NOT GOOGLE_ADS_CLIENT_ID or GOOGLE_ADS_CUSTOMER_ID. A client id is an identifier, not a secret, so this is the intended true-negative; but note an OAuth client secret's risk depends on pairing with the id, which name-shape detection does not model.
No financial/mutation inference: 'google-ads-mcp-server' does not match the financial or external-mutation hint lists, so only the env + supply-chain surfaces drive the verdict.
